scope: AgenC model-facing Multi-Agent V2 tool surface backed by the live agent control plane.

requiredBehaviors: The model-facing registry exposes spawn_agent, wait_agent, close_agent, followup_task, send_message, and list_agents through the v2 handler factory.
Each handler keeps strict argument validation and rejects removed compatibility fields.
edgeCases: Execution-injected call/session ids remain accepted by strict argument validation.
The extraction preserves the existing tool order before agent job tools.

requiredBehaviors: spawn_agent requires message and task_name, rejects fork_context, and parses fork_turns as none, all, or a positive integer string.
Role, model, reasoning effort, task name, and depth limits are validated before lifecycle events and delegation.
Successful async launches emit spawn begin/end events, register background task lifecycle output, increment telemetry, and return task_name plus optional nickname metadata.
edgeCases: Unknown models and unsupported reasoning efforts return model-facing errors before delegation.
Over-depth spawns return a model-facing error without emitting lifecycle events.
Configured metadata hiding returns only task_name.

requiredBehaviors: wait_agent emits waiting begin/end events and returns Wait completed or Wait timed out.
timeout_ms is optional, must be positive when provided, and is clamped by session minimum and maximum bounds.
Mailbox-change waiting is owned by the tool handler rather than the generic executor.
edgeCases: Sessions without waitForMailboxChange fall back to checking pending mailbox state after the timeout.

requiredBehaviors: close_agent resolves targets through the current agent path, rejects root, emits close begin/end events, shuts down the target, and returns previous_status.
Receiver nickname and role metadata are included when available.
edgeCases: Status subscription failures fall back to the control-plane status lookup before shutdown.

requiredBehaviors: send_message and followup_task share a text-only delivery path.
send_message queues delivery without triggering a target turn.
followup_task triggers the target turn and rejects assignments to the root agent.
Interaction begin/end events are emitted around delivery, including after delivery failures.
edgeCases: Empty messages are rejected before target resolution.
Unresolved targets and missing receiver agent paths return model-facing errors.

requiredBehaviors: list_agents returns AgenC snake_case entries with agent_name, agent_status, and optional last_task_message.
Optional path_prefix is resolved relative to the current agent path.
Unsupported role filters and other removed fields are rejected.
edgeCases: Completed statuses are rendered in the model-facing status shape.
